philippines.title: Thank You, Mom: Celebrate Mom the best way at Robinsons Malls
philippines.description:
This Mother’s Day, go beyond the usual and celebrate the extraordinary women who do it all. At Robinsons Land Corporation’s Robinsons Malls, families are invited to treat Mom to a thoughtfully curated day designed to let her pause, recharge, and feel appreciated. Beauty and Wellness Start her day with the gift of uninterrupted self-care. Treat […]...

philippines.title: Beyond Asean hosting: Pam wants delegates to return, invest in Cebu
philippines.description:
CEBU CITY, Philippines — Cebu Governor Pamela “Pam” Baricuatro wants the province to gain more than global exposure from the ongoing 48th Asean Summit and related meetings in Cebu. She said the real success of the event will come if foreign delegates, state leaders, and investors return to Cebu long after the summit ends. As […]...
Keep on reading: Beyond Asean hosting: Pam wants delegates to return, invest in Cebu

philippines.title: Lee Jae-wook to enlist in the military on May 18
philippines.description:
Lee Jae-wook, a South Korean actor best known for his lead role in “Alchemy of Souls,” will enlist in the military on May 18. The actor’s enlistment date was reported by Korean media outlet STARNEWS on May 4, which was confirmed by his agency Log Studio hours later. “Lee Jae-wook will be enlisting as an active duty soldier on May 18,” it said via a Soompi translation. In the weeks leading up to his enlistment, the actor has been filming for his upcoming medical drama “Doctor on the Edge,” scheduled for a June 1 premiere on Disney+. The series also […]...
Keep on reading: Lee Jae-wook to enlist in the military on May 18

energy.title: Oil Prices Waver as Market Weighs Chances of U.S.-Iran Deal
energy.description: Front-month Brent Crude futures dropped to below $100 per barrel again in morning trade in Europe on Thursday, reversing earlier gains, as the market hopes the U.S. and Iran could be able to reach an agreement that would eventually reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Following a steep selloff on Wednesday, which crashed the prices by 7%, Asian trade on Thursday began with nearly 1% gains for both benchmarks, amid investor concern that the U.S.-Iran deal is not as imminent as signals on Wednesday suggested. The continued blockage at the Strait of Hormuz…

energy.title: Oil Crunch Prompts Plastics Crisis in Asia
energy.description: Asia is facing plastics shortages because of the oil and gas supply crunch caused by the war in the Middle East, with medical supplies, packaging, and consumer products most vulnerable to a shock, the Financial Times has reported. The biggest problem for the world’s largest plastics-producing region is the shortage of naphtha, a key plastics feedstock. As a result of the supply squeeze, the price of the feedstock has doubled in Asia, leading to higher prices for a wide variety of products, contributing to already worrying inflation trends…
